subparts.text: What is a time-space trade-off? Provide an example. 2.5
How is a two-dimensional array represented in memory? 2.5
What are priority queues, and where are they used? 2.5
Explain circular linked lists with their applications. 2.5
Describe the concept of binary search trees with an example. 2.5
What is merge sort? Discuss its efficiency. 2.5
Explain the concept of spanning trees in graph theory. 2.5
What is collision resolution in hashing? Discuss one technique. 2.5

questions.question_text: Write an algorithm to count inversions in an array. An Invrsion in an array is a plain of indices such that i<j and arr{i}>arr{i}.
Describe the sorting of arrays using insertion sort. Write an algorithm/program to sort an array using this method.
What are queues? Discuss the difference between linear queues and circular queues with examples.
Explain the representation of stacks using linked lists. Write an algorithm to perform push and pop operations.
Insert the following elements into an AVL tree !100, 50, 150 25, 75, 125, 175, 10, 30, 60. After constructing the tree, delete the node with value 75. Show the rebalanced AVL tree after deletion.
Explain B-trees. Discuss their advantages and write the steps for insertion in a B-tree.
What is Dijkstra’s algorithm? Explain its working with an example.

Given a complete graph with 5 vertices 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and weighted edges, where the weight between any two vehicles is represented by the distance between them. Now find a minimum spanning tree for this graph using Prim’s and Krushal’s algorithms. Use distance as {i-j}, where i&j are vertices of the graph.
